talk_metadata.talk_title: Development of NEO811, a molecular glue degrader of ARNT, which is also HIF-1 beta, with potent single activity in clear cell renal cell carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't expect that our mechanism will avoid the well-established consequence such as anemia, but we believe that these are easily monitorable and manageable.
Speaker said: we have not disclosed all the biomarker strategy data at this time.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential redundancy of HIF-2α inhibition in tumors with low HIF-2α expression
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We haven't actually proven the mechanism in the XP490 model yet, but we hypothesize it may involve HIF1α signaling.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: We see full activity in some resistant models while belzutifan fails.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: No real changes in body weight despite systemic ARNT degradation, but anemia is expected.

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase 1 first‑in‑human study of NEO811 in locally advanced or metastatic ccRCC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all ccRCC patients regardless of HIF‑2α status or ARNT expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline tumor HIF‑2α or ARNT expression to stratify responders
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No on‑treatment ctDNA or serial biopsies to track ARNT degradation dynamics
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm design with limited patient numbers; no comparator arm

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Cereblon‑based molecular glue platform targeting ARNT
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed detailed Cryo‑EM structure, revealing a large conformational change that may be exploitable for alternative binders.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad pan‑HIF degradation may limit combination flexibility with other VHL‑targeted agents.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed Cryo‑EM data indicating a complex, multi‑domain induced degron, yet concluded that the approach will be broadly combinable with all downstream HIF inhibitors without addressing potential pathway cross‑talk.

key_data_gaps: No comparator to standard of care

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model the pan‑HIF degron interface to predict and design next‑generation glues that retain activity in HIF‑2α low tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker hypothesized that HIF‑1α may drive pathology in HIF‑2α low models and that ARNT degradation captures that biology.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's protein‑protein interaction prediction to identify alternative neo‑substrate motifs for ARNT that overcome low HIF‑2α dependency.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a companion diagnostic measuring tumor ARNT/HIF‑2α levels to enrich for patients likely to respond to NEO811.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted lack of patient selection based on HIF‑2α status and admitted uncertainty about mechanism in the XP490 model.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO's expression‑driven modeling to correlate ARNT degradation kinetics with baseline HIF signatures.

cited_competitors.name: belzutifan
cited_competitors.context: Compared as a HIF‑2α inhibitor that loses activity in G323E mutant; NEO811 retains activity.

cited_competitors.name: cabozantinib
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as an approved VEGFR TKI in the same pathway.

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism of tumor regression in HIF‑2α null models?
Will chronic systemic ARNT degradation cause unforeseen toxicities beyond anemia?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AZD8359, a novel CD8-based T-cell engager designed to deliver a wide therapeutic window in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't see any detrimental effect, even in durability, in all sorts of aspects of this reduced engagement of the CD4s.
Speaker said: we have not had any red flags in anything we've been doing that suggests that biasing to the CD8s is increasing our ADA risk.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential loss of CD4 helper function leading to reduced long‑term T‑cell memory
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: We see potent killing even without the CD8 arm, but potency is increased with it.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: No explicit mention of CRS at high doses, but preclinical data may not capture human cytokine storm risk.

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase 1 study of AZD8359 in metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: CD8‑biased T‑cell engagement to STEAP2‑expressing tumor cells
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all mCRPC patients without pre‑selecting for STEAP2 expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Tumor STEAP2 IHC or RNA expression to confirm target presence
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial cytokine monitoring plan described
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm dose‑escalation may not detect rare CRS events

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described the format but did not disclose unique engineering details, suggesting potential for competitors to replicate CD8 bias using alternative scaffolds.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without STEAP2 biomarker may dilute efficacy signal.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed in vitro data that CD4 T cells produce cytokines and contribute to antitumor activity, yet concluded that biasing to CD8 will reduce CRS without compromising efficacy.

key_data_gaps: No human safety data on CRS incidence
No head‑to‑head comparison with non‑biased TCEs in clinic

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can develop a predictive model for STEAP2 expression thresholds needed for optimal AZD8359 activity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized that STEAP2 homogenous high expression is critical for efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's tumor‑cell surface expression modeling to define cut‑offs for patient enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic agents that boost CD4 helper function without increasing CRS, complementing CD8 bias.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted durability studies suggest indirect CD4 activation is still present.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO to simulate immune network effects of adding checkpoint inhibitors or cytokine modulators.

cited_competitors.name: PSMA‑targeted TCEs
cited_competitors.context: Compared STEAP2 expression favorably against PSMA, citing lower normal tissue expression.

cited_competitors.name: Conventional 2+1 TCEs
cited_competitors.context: Used as benchmark for cytokine release and CD4 activation.

unresolved_questions: Will CD8 bias affect long‑term immune memory in patients?
What is the incidence of ADA formation in humans?

talk_metadata.talk_title: TNG961, a selective oral molecular glue degrader of HBS1L for the treatment of FOCAD deficient cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we have a favorable safety profile for TNG961 and achieved exposures 20‑fold above our human predicted efficacious dose.
Speaker said: we anticipate clinical activity as monotherapy, as well as we have observed combination benefit with PRMT5 inhibitors.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target degradation of GSPT1 at higher concentrations
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We haven't proven the mechanism in the XP490 model yet, but we hypothesize it may involve HIF1α signaling.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Complete regressions observed in some models while others only show stasis.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: GLP toxicology showed no overt toxicity, but long‑term ER stress effects are not yet known.

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: First‑in‑human dose escalation of TNG961 in FOCAD‑deleted solid tumors
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Synthetic lethal HBS1L degradation in FOCAD‑negative cancers
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all FOCAD‑deleted patients without confirming MTAP co‑deletion or SKI complex status
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: FOCAD loss confirmation method (IHC vs NGS) and SKI complex integrity
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial tumor biopsies planned to monitor HBS1L degradation over time
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early‑phase single‑arm design may mask variable responses across lineages

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Glutarimide‑based molecular glue degrader targeting HBS1L via cereblon
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed detailed cryo‑EM interactions, potentially enabling competitors to design analogues.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single synthetic lethal interaction may limit efficacy in heterogeneous tumors.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented proteomics showing exclusive HBS1L degradation, yet later admitted off‑target GSPT1 activity appears at higher concentrations.

key_data_gaps: Long‑term safety of chronic ER stress induction
Biomarker validation for patient selection beyond FOCAD loss
Comparative efficacy versus PRMT5 inhibitors in head‑to‑head trials

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can integrate FOCAD, MTAP, and SKI complex genomic data to define a high‑confidence patient cohort for TNG961.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that FOCAD loss co‑occurs with MTAP loss and that SKI complex destabilization predicts sensitivity.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's pathway‑level synthetic lethality modeling to prioritize patients with combined deletions.

crispro_opportunity.description: Model potential GSPT1 cross‑reactivity to design next‑generation glues with improved selectivity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned that some analogues picked up GSPT1 activity at nanomolar range.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO's off‑target prediction engine to redesign the degron interface.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify additional synergistic agents (e.g., KRAS inhibitors) that exploit translational stress induced by HBS1L loss.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker suggested broad safety profile could allow combination with many agents.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate combination networks to prioritize agents that enhance ER stress without overlapping toxicity.

cited_competitors.name: volapemetostat (PRMT5 inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Compared as a co‑deleted MTAP‑targeted therapy; shown non‑overlapping resistance with TNG961.

cited_competitors.name: sotorasib (KRAS G12C inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a potential combination partner due to favorable safety profile.

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal biomarker panel (FOCAD, MTAP, SKI complex) for patient selection?
Will chronic HBS1L degradation cause unacceptable ER stress in normal tissues?

talk_metadata.talk_title: EPI-326, a novel mutation‑independent and normal tissue‑sparing EGFR bispecific antibody that degrades EGFR for the treatment of EGFR‑driven solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we saw no EGFR‑related toxicities nor any toxicities at the maximal feasible dose of 204 mg/kg in non‑human primates.
Speaker said: the EGFR binder maintains normal signaling in skin keratinocytes, unlike current EGFR blockers.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Tumors lacking EGFR dependence may not respond despite EGFR degradation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We haven't proven why some EGFR‑low tumors still regress with EPI‑326.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Complete regressions observed in EGFR‑mutant models, but not quantified in EGFR‑independent models.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Potential TMDD not observed in cyno, but human TMDD remains uncertain.

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase 1 dose‑escalation of EPI‑326 in EGFR‑driven NSCLC, HNSCC, and CRC
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Bispecific EGFR degradation via integrin β6‑mediated internalization
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all EGFR‑driven cancers without confirming integrin β6 co‑expression
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Tumor integrin β6 IHC or RNA to ensure targetable degrading receptor presence
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No planned serial tumor biopsies to monitor EGFR degradation kinetics
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm design may mask lack of activity in integrin‑low subpopulations

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: EpiTac bispecific antibody platform using integrin β6 as degrading receptor
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described the receptor selection process; the underlying bispecific scaffold is a conventional IgG1, potentially replicable.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on co‑expression of integrin β6 limits applicability to tumors with low β6 levels.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that EGFR blockade alone only yields tumor stasis, yet concluded that degradation will be curative across all EGFR‑driven tumors without presenting data in EGFR‑independent contexts.

key_data_gaps: Human PK/PD correlation for EGFR degradation duration
Biomarker validation of integrin β6 expression in patient tumors
Long‑term safety beyond 3 doses in primates

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to model integrin β6 expression thresholds needed for effective EGFR degradation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized high and homogeneous integrin β6 expression in tumor types as a key selection criterion.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict patient subsets with sufficient β6 co‑expression to prioritize enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Demonstrate via CrisPRO that extracellular bispecific degradation removes EGFR scaffolding functions unavailable to small‑molecule inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that degradation reduces phospho‑HER3 and c‑Jun more than blocking antibodies.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Quantify loss of downstream signaling networks after EGFR removal versus inhibition.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic agents (e.g., KRAS inhibitors) that complement EGFR degradation in KRAS‑mutant EGFR‑expressing tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed preclinical synergy of EPI‑326 with sotorasib in CRC models.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model pathway cross‑talk to prioritize combination regimens.

cited_competitors.name: Cetuximab
cited_competitors.context: Compared as a conventional EGFR blocker that causes dose‑limiting toxicities.

cited_competitors.name: Osimertinib
cited_competitors.context: Used as a combination partner in NSCLC models.

cited_competitors.name: Sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: Combined with EPI‑326 in CRC PDX models showing deep responses.

unresolved_questions: Will EGFR degradation be sustained in patients with high EGFR turnover?
What is the incidence of anti‑drug antibodies (ADA) with repeated dosing?
